title: Pulse Field Gel Electrophoresis for Long Read Sequencing
author: Benjamin Schwessinger
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.83jhykn

steps.title: Prepare 2 L of 0
steps.instruction: Prepare 2 L of 0.5x TBE buffer, along with 100 ml of 0.5x TBE buffer in a separate container.

steps.title: Add 1g of Agarose (I/Ultrapure) to the 100 ml 0
steps.instruction: Add 1g of Agarose (I/Ultrapure) to the 100 ml 0.5x TBE, to make a 1% agarose gel. Heat and mix until homogenous. Add 10 µl SYBR safe dye and mix again.

steps.title: Transfer 200 ul of the 1%...
steps.instruction: Transfer 200 ul of the 1% gel solution to a separate Eppendorf tube. Keep this tube heated at 60ºC on a heat block.

steps.title: Pour the 2L of 0
steps.instruction: Pour the 2L of 0.5x TBE into the central tray of the PFGE container.

steps.title: Switch on the power and pump...
steps.instruction: Switch on the power and pump switches on the main CHEF unit.

steps.title: Switch on the power for the...
steps.instruction: Switch on the power for the cooling module and set it to 14ºC.

steps.title: Set the variable speed pump to 55
steps.instruction: Set the variable speed pump to 55. Wait until container current temperature is 14 degrees before adding gel.

steps.title: The gel has 15 wells; it...
steps.instruction: The gel has 15 wells; it is generally advisable to use at least 2 sets of ladders on both sides of the samples (4 wells for ladders). Remove the well comb and add (less than 1 mm) any gel-stored ladders to the gel while it is still in the casting tray.

steps.title: Add the warm 200 µl of...
steps.instruction: Add the warm 200 µl of agarose over the gel plug ladders. Wait for these to cool.

steps.title: When the current temperature of the...
steps.instruction: When the current temperature of the TBE buffer is around 14 ºC, switch off the pump and cooling system.

steps.title: Add 300 ng of DNA (or...
steps.instruction: Add 300 ng of DNA (or as close to) for each sample to the prepared tube, and make up volume to 16 µl with the buffer the DNA is stored in.

steps.title: For each sample, prepare a PCR...
steps.instruction: For each sample, prepare a PCR tube with 4 µl of loading dye.

steps.title: Unscrew the front and rear sides...
steps.instruction: Unscrew the front and rear sides of the casting tray, and place the gel in the central slot of the electrophoresis cell.

steps.title: Load the samples and any liquid ladders
steps.instruction: Load the samples and any liquid ladders.

steps.title: Set the program 1 and start the run
steps.instruction: Set the program 1 and start the run.

steps.title: Wait for around 10 minutes, and...
steps.instruction: Wait for around 10 minutes, and then verify that the gel is running (the dye should run into the gel at this point)

steps.title: Switch the pump and cooling module back on
steps.instruction: Switch the pump and cooling module back on. Set the pump to around 80 and the cooling module to 14ºC.

steps.title: After the program has finished, remove...
steps.instruction: After the program has finished, remove the gel and place it in a suitable tray. (Find the smallest container which fits the gel but is large enough to comfortably slip the gel with gel backplate in and out.)

steps.title: Image the gel as usual
steps.instruction: Image the gel as usual.

steps.title: Dispose of the gel and rinse...
steps.instruction: Dispose of the gel and rinse and dry the gel backplate.

steps.title: Acquire a large bucket or other...
steps.instruction: Acquire a large bucket or other liquid container (2L or more recommended). Place this below the output port of the electrophoresis cell. (marked "TUBE TO CHILLER")

steps.title: Acquire a second large bucket (2-5L),...
steps.instruction: Acquire a second large bucket (2-5L), and fill it with RO/DI water. Place this in a stable location within reach of the input tube to the cooling module.

steps.title: Disconnect the tube leading to the cooling module
steps.instruction: Disconnect the tube leading to the cooling module. The buffer should start draining into the container under the output port.

steps.title: Place the tube leading to the...
steps.instruction: Place the tube leading to the cooling module into the clean water container, and turn the pump on.

steps.title: Ensure that the CHEF program is...
steps.instruction: Ensure that the CHEF program is inactive and that the pump and cooling module are switched off.

steps.title: As needed, drain the output container...
steps.instruction: As needed, drain the output container and fill the clean water container until about 6 L of water has been flushed through the system.

steps.title: Place a drainage container under the...
steps.instruction: Place a drainage container under the rear input port (from the pump) of the electrophoresis cell. Disconnect the pump tube and let the remaining flush water drain from the electrophoresis cell.

steps.title: Leave the tubes disconnected and the...
steps.instruction: Leave the tubes disconnected and the cell lid ajar to dry. If not in use for an extended period, return in a week to reconnect tubes and cell lid to prevent dust build up.

steps.title: Disconnect the tube between the cooling module and pump
steps.instruction: Disconnect the tube between the cooling module and pump.

steps.title: Pour the remaining molten gel into the casting tray
steps.instruction: Pour the remaining molten gel into the casting tray. Allow to cool/ solidify without disruption.

steps.title: Assemble the casting tray, ensuring that...
steps.instruction: Assemble the casting tray, ensuring that the screws are tightened to make the setup watertight. Add the gel comb to the comb slot closest to the edge. Casting tray components. The assembled casting tray. Slots on the side are for the gel comb.
